articles.title: James May Says Tesla Model 3 Overcomplicates Basic Controls, Wiper 'Doesn't Work Properly'
articles.summary: British motoring journalist James May criticized the Tesla Model 3 for overcomplicating basic controls, highlighting issues like the multi-step process for driver assistance settings and the ineffectiveness of the camera-based automatic wipers. While praising its quietness and performance, he expressed dislike for Tesla's association with Elon Musk and noted slow charging speeds. Despite these criticisms, the Tesla Model Y, which shares similarities with the Model 3, was the best-selling vehicle globally from 2023-2025.

articles.title: Tesla faces deeper U.S. probe over Full Self-Driving safety concerns (TSLA:NASDAQ)
articles.summary: Federal safety regulators, specifically the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA), are intensifying their probe into Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) system due to concerns about its performance in adverse roadway conditions. The investigation will focus on FSD's ability to handle issues like sun glare, fog, and dust, which have been linked to crashes and fatalities. The NHTSA's findings could lead to recalls or enforcement actions, potentially impacting Tesla's operations and reputation.

articles.title: Musk says Tesla may 'tape out' next-generation AI6 chips in December
articles.summary: Elon Musk, CEO of Tesla, has indicated that the company might finalize the design ("tape out") for its next-generation AI6 chips by December. These chips, which Samsung Electronics is expected to produce using an advanced 2-nanometer process by the second half of 2027, are intended for use in Tesla's self-driving cars and humanoid robots. The announcement follows Samsung's $16.5 billion deal to supply AI chips to Tesla.

articles.title: Musk says SpaceX AI, Tesla will keep ordering Nvidia chips at scale
articles.summary: Elon Musk has stated that his companies, SpaceX AI and Tesla, will continue to place large orders for Nvidia chips. This announcement was made late on Thursday, underscoring the ongoing demand for Nvidia's technology in advanced AI and automotive applications. The statement suggests continued reliance of Musk's ventures on Nvidia for their technological needs.

articles.title: Forget Tesla — Nvidia And Lucid Are Quietly Building Uber's AI Fleet
articles.summary: Nvidia and Lucid are reportedly collaborating to build an autonomous vehicle fleet for Uber, offering a modular competitor to Tesla's vertically integrated robotaxi strategy. Nvidia provides the AI and computing power, while Lucid is developing autonomy-ready vehicles for scaled deployment. Uber's existing global demand and distribution network are seen as crucial to the success of this partnership, potentially shifting the focus of the robotaxi race from individual car makers to ecosystem builders.

articles.title: Kurv Yield Premium Strategy Tesla (TSLA) ETF (TSLP) Dividend Yield 2026 & History $TSLP
articles.summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the Kurv Yield Premium Strategy Tesla (TSLA) ETF (TSLP) dividend information. It highlights TSLP's significant annual dividend yield of 46.19% and an annual dividend of $8.96 per share, with the most recent payment being $1.30 per share on January 24, 2025. The content also includes historical dividend data, payment schedules, and a comparison of TSLP's dividend performance against other companies and BATS companies.
